signals.title: NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme
signals.text: RBNZ 2.5%. The +250bp carry premium favors NZD, while CHF remains the G10 laggard amid easing Middle East tensions. NZD COT LF net short -27,675 (long% 18.0%) represents a significant positioning extreme; a hawkish RBNZ delivery on 2 Sep triggers a crowded short-unwind. Retail 86% short AUD/CHF confirms a broader anti-CHF sentiment among retail participants.
signals.watch_level: Watch for RBNZ meeting on 2 Sep to confirm hike delivery

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Vol + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.42 — The pair holds above the critical 160.00 threshold where BoJ verbal warnings previously intensified. A +275bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.98%) sustains the bid despite USD net short positioning (lev_net -4,463). Options market shows elevated hedging demand with IV at 7.2% vs HV30 at 3.5% (VRP +3.7%), while 25d RR at -1.37 confirms a moderate put skew as traders pay for downside protection against potential BoJ intervention.
signals.watch_level: 160.00 remains the key psychological floor for intervention risk

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + CESI Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1452 — Bearish momentum builds as EUR CESI (-15 net misses) diverges from USD CESI (+51 net beats), signaling persistent Eurozone data disappointment. COT LF net short -66,057 (long% 34.7%) aligns with the negative data trend. While ECB (2.25%) is in a hiking cycle vs Fed (3.75%) on hold, the +145bp OIS carry advantage favors the USD. Options VRP at +2.1% indicates the market is pricing in higher volatility than realized, favoring USD on any energy-driven inflation shocks.
signals.watch_level: Watch ECB meeting on 23 Jul for shift in hiking rhetoric

signals.title: GBP/USD — Retail vs Institutional Divergence
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3487 — A sharp divergence is active as retail sentiment is 72% short while COT LF net remains long +17,228 (59.0%). UK GDP growth of +0.1% in May supports the BoE's 3.75% hold stance, but BBH notes hikes are not viewed as bullish given weak manufacturing. GBP 1W avg performance of +0.568% makes it a G10 leader, yet a high VRP of +5.0% (IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.0%) suggests institutional caution and expensive upside protection.
signals.watch_level: Watch for retail capitulation if price tests 1.3550

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + BoC Hold
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4032 — The historical link between CAD and Oil is broken (z = -1.96σ), with USD/CAD vs WTI correlation at -0.926 vs a near-zero norm. The BoC's decision to hold at 2.25% and remove hike language removes a key CAD tailwind, leaving the pair sensitive to the +150bp USD carry advantage. CAD COT positioning is heavily short (lev_net -87,846, long% 22.6%), suggesting the move higher is well-anticipated by speculators.
signals.watch_level: Sustained break above 1.4100 confirms CAD weakness regime
